riddle:
1. Seek the early newspaper illustrator who supplied gag panels and single‑panel comics; on that page, note the short phrase his biography credits as his chief, notable contribution.

2. There exists a line showing his full name; count how many vowels it contains (a, e, i, o, u — tally every occurrence) and keep that count as your next numeric result.

3. Which comic strip title appears in the image caption that shows Jack Elrod at work — read the caption and write down the strip name you find there.

4. If you consult the article about record packaging and inspect the formats section, report the standard outside dimensions for a cardboard LP sleeve in inches, expressed as width × height.

5. The article that chronicles the Australian prize for album‑art design records when the category for best cover visuals was first given; locate and record that inaugural year.

6. In that narrative the year you just discovered contains a simple mathematical curiosity: determine how many positive divisors (factors) that year has, counting 1 and the year itself.

7. The island continent speaks like a census: locate a reliable source and note the current population figure given for Australia — record that whole number.

8. (Within the entry about the pub‑born rock movement, read the section titled where the “first bands emerge” are discussed and count how many groups are listed as pioneering the early‑1970s sound.)

9. Again, retrieve the nation's population figure for Australia from the same authoritative source and record that number a second time.

10. Finally, using only the numeric answers you obtained above, compute the following expression and give its single‑digit result: (clue 6 + clue 7 + clue 9) modulo 10.

metadata.theme: From cartoonist to cover art: tracing how visual illustrators (like Clarence D. Russell) influenced popular visual culture — moving from newspaper cartoons to album-cover design, music awards and rock culture — with small data checks (unit conversion, population, travel time) to ground the story.
